Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user asks Consider an event log stored in a DuckDB table named event_log with columns case_id activity timestamp A process variant is defined as the ordered sequence of activities that occur in a single case determined by sorting events within that case by their timestamp

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to produce a SQL query that does exactly that The user did not specify K we could set a variable like K equals 3 or maybe use a parameter We might need to join back to original table

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: One approach using list agg ordered to create the sequence for each case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then group by that sequence to get variant counts

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now we have top K sequences Then join back to original event_log to get all events for those case_ids

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Alternatively use a CTE to make it readable

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Also we need to ensure that sequence is represented as array maybe for readability

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: We can also use string agg function or list agg to aggregate In DuckDB we can use list agg activity ordered by timestamp which returns a list but to compare sequences we might use array But we can also use string concatenation In DuckDB there is array agg and list agg There is also string agg

Name: Validation - PE
Text: For K maybe use a constant or parameter We can set K equals 5 for example

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The final answer will explain the logic and then present a complete query with CTEs which is readable

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We need to emphasize that we use list agg which orders by timestamp generating ordered activity list
